CDISC Definition: Terminology for the test codes concerned with the measurement of organs.
NCI Preferred Term: CDISC SEND Organ Measurement Test Code Terminology

CDISC Definition: The length of the closed curve of a circle; the size of something as given by the distance around it. (NCI)
The linear extent in space from one end of something to the other end, or the extent of something from beginning to end. (NCI)
A numeric comparison of the weight of an organ to the weight of the brain.
A numeric comparison of the weight of an organ to body weight.
A numeric comparison of the weight of an organ to the weight of the heart.
The dimension between two surfaces of an object, usually the smallest dimension as opposed to the width or the length.
The mean number in a group of values that represent the thickness of an object.
A measurement of the amount of three dimensional space occupied by an object or the capacity of a space or container.
The vertical force exerted by a mass as a result of gravity. (NCI)
